notes: From what I tested, it seems everything worked. All audios, musics, and effects. Controller connection and disconnection was detected immediately, force feedback worked (does not work on Windows). There is a performance hit, but it did not seem too bad, probably would be smaller if Gallium Nine was usable.
title: Child of Light

notes:
Tried with Disable Esync but does not work

Uplay client appears and downloads an update. Once finished it attempts to restart and apply the update, but is unable to do so. Uplay client does not appear on subsequent launch attempts and instead seems to create a zombie process.
title: Child of Light

notes: Game runs very well. No tweaks applied. However, Uplay needs to be restarted every time since the game does only start when using the "Play"-Button within Steam, which first starts Uplay and Child of Light automatically afterwards. Within Uplay itself there is only a "Download"-button available which doesn't seem to do anything. Three minor issues: 1: I can't get rid of the box in the upper right corner "Press Shift+F2 while in game to activate the overlay" while in game. 2: there is a black square appearing at the center of the screen at the beginning of each fight which fades away quickly. So it doesn't interfere with the gameplay. 3: When my Xbox 360 wireless controller disconnected due to low battery, I had to restart the game to be able to use it again.
title: Child of Light

notes: Uplay might lock up on the first run and need to be manually terminated from the system monitor, but the game launches successfully on subsequent attempts. Good performance and full controller support in-game and I noted no obvious glitches or errors in 1.5 hours of play. When quitting the game the Uplay process does not end properly and again needs to be stopped using the system monitor.

notes: The game works fine, but I had to install a new version of Uplay via protontricks (shell command: protontricks 256290 uplay) to make it run.

title: Child of Light

notes:
Game launches fine but for the first time, you'll need to go to the wine window that launches uplay, go to settings and disable the uplay overlay. Subsequent launches will show a warning about the uplay overlay disabled. Simply click to proceed to the game. 

Achievements for uplay still work as usual and the steam overlay works fine in game too. Game runs perfectly except for one graphical glitch at the beginning of battles where you see a visible black box appear for a second before disappearing.

I assume this can be fixed. Not sure if it happens on mesa though. 

I'm using the NVIDIA proprietary driver.
title: Child of Light

notes:
When you first launch it, nothing happens. Uplay won't start. You have to kill upc.exe and it will then start with Child of Light. You then ensure that the overlay is disabled via the uplay settings.

There is a black square appearing at the center of the screen at the beginning of each fight which fades away quickly. This is due to a glitch it seems with battle intros. Go to game settings and disable battle intros and the problem goes away.
title: Child of Light

notes: Uplay starts and logs in fine, but the game crashes after a few seconds of white screen. The log just says page fault, I couldn't find any more useful information.
title: Child of Light

notes: starting/uplay is a bit slow sometimes (appeared to be stuck in checking for patches for the game one time), but after starting the game everything seems to be working properly; no changes/settings to the default proton config were need
title: Child of Light
